summary: Postinstall script exfiltrates SSH keys and credentials to hardcoded remote IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes shell commands that read `/root/.ssh/*` (including private keys via `cat`), `/root/.npm/_cacache` JSON contents, `/home/ubuntu` files, environment variables matching `flag`/`htb`, and greps the filesystem for `HTB{...}` markers. The collected data is base64-encoded and sent via HTTP PUT to `http://154.57.164.64:32271`. Execution is gated to evade analysis: it returns early when popular npm mirrors or scanners are detected (`npmmirror`, `tencent`, `hscan`, `ustc`, `huawei`, `cnpm`, `taobao`), when CI environment variables are present (`GITHUB_ACTIONS`, `RUNNER_OS`, `CI`), or when cwd is `/tmp`; it only proceeds when the hostname matches a 12-hex Docker container ID and cwd contains `/app`. The combination of installer-secret theft, hardcoded attacker IP, and explicit scanner/CI evasion is the canonical malicious supply-chain shape.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
